:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Release Eligibility Determined?

Iowa is an indeterminate sentencing state; the judge has discretion in imposing the sentence to be served by the defendant and the Board has great discretion in determining release. Judges may impose a maximum sentence, but aside from mandatory sentences/mandatory minimum sentences imposed for certain specific crimes, there are no minimum sentences. Thus, most Iowa inmates are technically eligible for parole upon incarceration.
